Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer of Pezeka Credit Ltd.

Loan.title: Loan
Loan.description: Represents a loan disbursed to a customer.

loanTerm.description: Duration of the loan in months.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Represents a payment made by a customer towards a loan.

FinancialRecord.title: FinancialRecord
FinancialRecord.description: Represents a financial record of daily expenses, payouts, or receipts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the financial record.

payouts.description: Amount of daily payouts (customer disbursements with facilitation/transaction costs).

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the PezekaTrack application.

role.description: Role of the user (e.g., admin, finance, staff).

definition.description: Stores customer data. The 'customerId' is used to uniquely identify each customer.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

definition.description: Stores loan data for each customer. Includes denormalized 'customerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

params.name: loanId
params.description: The unique identifier for the loan.

definition.description: Stores payment data for each loan. Includes denormalized 'loanId' and 'customerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

params.name: loanId
params.description: The unique identifier for the loan.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the payment.

definition.description: Stores financial records. Access restricted to users with 'finance' role.

params.name: financialRecordId
params.description: The unique identifier for the financial record.

definition.description: Stores user data. Used for authentication and authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support PezekaTrack's core features, focusing on loan management, financial record keeping, and user authentication. The structure prioritizes authorization independence and facilitates secure list operations.  

**Authorization Independence:**  Each data entry contains all the information needed for access control, avoiding hierarchical `get()` calls.

*   **Customers:** Reside in `/customers/{customerId}`. Access to customer data is governed by the user's role.
*   **Loans:** Stored in `/customers/{customerId}/loans/{loanId}`. To achieve authorization independence, each loan document includes the `customerId`. This allows security rules to validate loan creation based on the `customerId` in the request and ensures that only users with appropriate permissions can access or modify loans associated with a customer.
*   **Payments:** Located under `/customers/{customerId}/loans/{loanId}/payments/{paymentId}`. Similarly, payment documents contain the `loanId` and (denormalized) `customerId` for independent authorization. Security rules can enforce that payments are only created, read, updated, or deleted by authorized users associated with the specific loan and customer.
*   **Financial Records:** Stored in `/financialRecords/{financialRecordId}`. Access to financial records requires a specific role, such as `finance`, which is verified in the security rules.  

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**  The design uses structural segregation to enable secure `list` operations.

*   Customers and Financial Records are in separate top-level collections based on authorization requirements. This segregation ensures that listing customers or financial records can be done securely, as different collections have different access control rules.
*   Loans and Payments are subcollections of Customers. Since a user must have access to a Customer to manage their Loans/Payments, and because of the authorization independence achieved by denormalizing the `customerId`, listing operations at these levels are secure.

This structure ensures data integrity, scalability, and debuggability while adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates.
